title: Add Repairs by Stage and Top Failing Models charts to Dashboard

requirements.summary: Add TanStack Query hook for fetching repairs-by-stage aggregation data
acceptanceCriteria: A useRepairsByStage hook exists that calls the backend aggregation query and returns stage/count data.
The hook follows the same pattern as existing query hooks in useQueries.ts.

file_operations.description: Add a useRepairsByStage hook that calls actor.getStageCounts() and returns the StageCount data. Follow the existing pattern of useQuery hooks in this file, using queryKey ['repairsByStage'] and enabling the query only when actor is available.

requirements.summary: Add TanStack Query hook for fetching top-failing-models aggregation data
acceptanceCriteria: A useTopFailingModels hook exists that calls the backend aggregation query and returns model/count data.
The hook follows the same pattern as existing query hooks in useQueries.ts.

file_operations.description: Add a useTopFailingModels hook that calls actor.getDeviceModelCounts() and returns the ModelCount data. Follow the existing pattern of useQuery hooks in this file, using queryKey ['topFailingModels'] and enabling the query only when actor is available.

requirements.summary: Add 'Repairs by Stage' vertical bar chart to DashboardPage with green bars, rotated X-axis labels, and live data
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Repairs by Stage' bar chart is visible on the dashboard page.
Bars are rendered in green.
All nine stage names appear on the X-axis, rotated for readability.
Chart data reflects live counts from actual backend repair ticket records.
Chart is wrapped in a styled card with the title 'Repairs by Stage'.
Chart displays a loading skeleton while data is being fetched.

file_operations.description: Create a new component RepairsByStageChart that uses the useRepairsByStage hook to fetch stage counts. Render a Recharts BarChart with vertical bars in green (#10b981). Transform the StageCount object into an array with keys for all nine stages (awaitingParts, closed, deployed, diagnosing, programming, qaTesting, readyDeploy, received, repairing) and their counts. Configure the X-axis with labels rotated at an angle (e.g., -45 degrees) using textAnchor='end' and angle={-45}. Display a loading skeleton when data is loading. Wrap the chart in a card with the title 'Repairs by Stage' using existing card styling patterns.

Import and integrate the RepairsByStageChart component into the dashboard layout. Position the chart below the existing KPI cards and above the recent records tables, in a new two-column grid row alongside the TopFailingModelsChart (REQ-6). Use a responsive grid layout that stacks charts vertically on smaller screens.

requirements.summary: Add 'Top Failing Models' horizontal bar chart to DashboardPage with orange bars and live data
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Top Failing Models' horizontal bar chart is visible on the dashboard page.
Bars are rendered in orange.
Model names appear on the Y-axis.
Chart data reflects live counts from actual backend repair ticket records.
Chart is wrapped in a styled card with the title 'Top Failing Models'.
Chart displays a loading skeleton while data is being fetched.

file_operations.description: Create a new component TopFailingModelsChart that uses the useTopFailingModels hook to fetch model counts. Render a Recharts BarChart with layout='vertical' and horizontal bars in orange (#f59e0b). Transform the ModelCount object into an array with keys for the five models (vx680, vx820, m400, carbon10, carbon8) and their counts, sorted by count descending. Configure the Y-axis to display model names (VX680, VX820, M400, Carbon 10, Carbon 8) and the X-axis to show repair counts. Display a loading skeleton when data is loading. Wrap the chart in a card with the title 'Top Failing Models' using existing card styling patterns.

Import and integrate the TopFailingModelsChart component into the dashboard layout. Position the chart in the same two-column grid row as the RepairsByStageChart (REQ-5), below the KPI cards and above the recent records tables.

requirements.summary: Lay out the two new charts side-by-side in a two-column grid row on the DashboardPage
acceptanceCriteria: Both charts appear in the same horizontal row on the dashboard.
The layout uses a two-column grid (each chart taking roughly equal width).
On smaller screens the layout stacks the charts vertically.
The charts are positioned below KPI cards and above the recent records tables.

file_operations.description: Create a new section in the DashboardPage below the KPI cards and above the recent records tables. Use a responsive grid layout (e.g., grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2 gap-6) to display the RepairsByStageChart and TopFailingModelsChart side-by-side on larger screens and stacked vertically on smaller screens. Ensure both charts have equal width in the grid and proper spacing.
